title: Godfather of AI: They Keep Silencing Me But I'm Trying to ...
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Comments 24K
Content:
196K Likes 8,674,686 Views Jun 16 2025 He pioneered AI, now he’s warning the world. Godfather of AI Geoffrey Hinton breaks his silence on the deadly dangers of AI no one is prepared for. Geoffrey Hinton is a leading computer scientist and cognitive psychologist, widely recognised as the ‘Godfather of AI’ for his pioneering work on neural networks and deep learning. He received the 2018 Turing Award, often called the Nobel Prize of computing. In 2023, he left Google to warn people about the rising dangers of AI. He explains: ◽️ Why there’s a real 20% chance AI could lead to HUMAN EXTINCTION. ◽️ How speaking out about AI got him SILENCED. ◽️ The deep REGRET he feels for helping create AI. ◽️ The 6 DEADLY THREATS AI poses to humanity right now. ◽️ AI’s potential to advance healthcare, boost productivity, and transform education. — ⏱ Timestamps: 00:00 Intro 02:11 Why Do They Call You the Godfather of AI?

title: Geoffrey Hinton | Biography, Nobel Prize, Machine Learning, AI, & Facts | Britannica
excerpts:
Section Title: Geoffrey Hinton
Content:
[SUBSCRIBE](https://premium.britannica.com/premium-membership/?utm_source=premium&utm_medium=inline-cta&utm_campaign=basics-2026)
Hinton has received [extensive](https://www.britannica.com/dictionary/extensive) recognition for his role in revolutionizing AI. Among his numerous awards are the Cognitive Science Society’s first-ever David E. Rumelhart Prize (2001) and the [Gerhard Herzberg](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Gerhard-Herzberg) Canada Gold Medal (2010), the country’s highest award for [science](https://www.britannica.com/science/science) and engineering. In 2018 Hinton was named a joint recipient of the [Turing Award](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Turing-Award) , often described as the “Nobel Prize of Computing,” for his breakthrough research on neural networks, and four years later he received the [Royal Society](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Royal-Society) ’s Royal Medal for his pioneering work on deep learning.
Michael McDo
